name: adafruit-circuitpython-onewire

metadata.name: adafruit-circuitpython-onewire
metadata.summary: CircuitPython OneWire helper library for 1-wire bus devices.

documentation:
# Introduction

```{image} https://readthedocs.org/projects/adafruit-circuitpython-onewire/badge/?version=latest
:alt: Documentation Status
:target: https://docs.circuitpython.org/projects/onewire/en/latest/
```

```{image} https://raw.githubusercontent.com/adafruit/Adafruit_CircuitPython_Bundle/main/badges/adafruit_discord.svg
:alt: Discord
:target: https://adafru.it/discord
```

```{image} https://github.com/adafruit/Adafruit_CircuitPython_OneWire/workflows/Build%20CI/badge.svg
:alt: Build Status
:target: https://github.com/adafruit/Adafruit_CircuitPython_OneWire/actions/
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/badge/code%20style-black-000000.svg
:alt: 'Code Style: Black'
:target: https://github.com/psf/black
```

Classes for use in communicating with devices on a 1-Wire bus.

# Dependencies

This driver depends on:

- [Adafruit CircuitPython](https://github.com/adafruit/circuitpython)

Please ensure all dependencies are available on the CircuitPython filesystem.
This is easily achieved by downloading
[the Adafruit library and driver bundle](https://github.com/adafruit/Adafruit_CircuitPython_Bundle).

**Note:** This library does **not** work on the Raspberry Pi as there is no kernel interface for it

# Usage Example

```python
import board
from adafruit_onewire.bus import OneWireBus
ow_bus = OneWireBus(board.D2)
devices = ow_bus.scan()
for d in devices:
    print("ROM={}\tFamily=0x{:02x}".format(d.rom, d.family_code))
```

# Documentation

API documentation for this library can be found on [Read the Docs](https://docs.circuitpython.org/projects/onewire/en/latest/).

For information on building library documentation, please check out [this guide](https://learn.adafruit.com/creating-and-sharing-a-circuitpython-library/sharing-our-docs-on-readthedocs#sphinx-5-1).

# Contributing

Contributions are welcome! Please read our [Code of Conduct](https://github.com/adafruit/Adafruit_CircuitPython_OneWire/blob/main/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md)
before contributing to help this project stay welcoming.

modules.name: adafruit_onewire.device
documentation:
`adafruit_onewire.device`
====================================================

Provides access to a single device on the 1-Wire bus.

* Author(s): Carter Nelson
SPDX-FileCopyrightText: 2017 Carter Nelson for Adafruit Industries
SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT

classes.name: adafruit_onewire.device.OneWireDevice

methods.name: adafruit_onewire.device.OneWireDevice.readinto
documentation:
Read into `buf` from the device. The number of bytes read will be the
length of `buf`.

If `start` or `end` is provided, then the buffer will be sliced
as if `buf[start:end]`. This will not cause an allocation like
`buf[start:end]` will so it saves memory.

```
param WriteableBuffer buf

Buffer to write into

param int start

Index to start writing at

param int end

Index to write up to but not include
```

methods.name: adafruit_onewire.device.OneWireDevice.write
documentation:
Write the bytes from `buf` to the device.

If `start` or `end` is provided, then the buffer will be sliced
as if `buffer[start:end]`. This will not cause an allocation like
`buffer[start:end]` will so it saves memory.

```
param ReadableBuffer buf

buffer containing the bytes to write

param int start

Index to start writing from

param int end

Index to read up to but not include
```
A class to represent a single device on the 1-Wire bus.

modules.name: adafruit_onewire.bus
documentation:
`adafruit_onewire.bus`
====================================================

Provide access to a 1-Wire bus.

* Author(s): Carter Nelson
SPDX-FileCopyrightText: 2017 Carter Nelson for Adafruit Industries
SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT

classes.name: adafruit_onewire.bus.OneWireError
documentation: A class to represent a 1-Wire exception.

classes.name: adafruit_onewire.bus.OneWireAddress

methods.name: adafruit_onewire.bus.OneWireAddress.rom
documentation: The unique 64 bit ROM code.

methods.name: adafruit_onewire.bus.OneWireAddress.crc
documentation: The 8 bit CRC.

methods.name: adafruit_onewire.bus.OneWireAddress.serial_number
documentation: The 48 bit serial number.

methods.name: adafruit_onewire.bus.OneWireAddress.family_code
documentation: The 8 bit family code.
A class to represent a 1-Wire address.

classes.name: adafruit_onewire.bus.OneWireBus

methods.name: adafruit_onewire.bus.OneWireBus.maximum_devices
documentation:
The maximum number of devices the bus will scan for. Valid range is 1 to 255.
It is an error to have more devices on the bus than this number. Having less is OK.

methods.name: adafruit_onewire.bus.OneWireBus.reset
documentation:
Perform a reset and check for presence pulse.

:param bool required: require presence pulse

methods.name: adafruit_onewire.bus.OneWireBus.readinto
documentation:
Read into `buf` from the device. The number of bytes read will be the
length of `buf`.

If `start` or `end` is provided, then the buffer will be sliced
as if `buf[start:end]`. This will not cause an allocation like
`buf[start:end]` will so it saves memory.

```
param ~WriteableBuffer buf

Buffer to write into

param int start

Index to start writing at

param int end

Index to write up to but not include
```

methods.name: adafruit_onewire.bus.OneWireBus.write
documentation:
Write the bytes from `buf` to the device.

If `start` or `end` is provided, then the buffer will be sliced
as if `buffer[start:end]`. This will not cause an allocation like
`buffer[start:end]` will so it saves memory.

```
param ReadableBuffer buf

Buffer containing the bytes to write

param int start

Index to start writing from

param int end

Index to read up to but not include
```

methods.name: adafruit_onewire.bus.OneWireBus.scan
documentation: Scan for devices on the bus and return a list of addresses.

methods.name: adafruit_onewire.bus.OneWireBus.crc8
documentation:
Perform the 1-Wire CRC check on the provided data.

:param ReadableBuffer data: 8 byte array representing 64 bit ROM code
A class to represent a 1-Wire bus.
